description:
You are a network engineer reviewing the results of several `ping` tests that have already been saved for you in the file
    /home/user/network/ping_report.log  

Your goal is to quickly identify every IPv4 address that ACTUALLY ANSWERED (i.e. produced a successful reply) and put the cleaned-up list in its own file so it can be shared with another team.

Definition of a “successful reply” for this task  
• A log line that begins with the literal text `64 bytes from` (no leading spaces).  
• The IPv4 address appears immediately after the word `from` and before the colon (`:`).  
• Example:  
  `64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=0 ttl=117 time=11.813 ms` → success from 8.8.8.8.

Deliverables you must create in the **same** directory (/home/user/network):  
1. /home/user/network/successful_ips.txt  
   • Each successful IPv4 address on its own line.  
   • No duplicates.  
   • Lines must be sorted in ascending numeric order (1.1.1.1 comes before 8.8.8.8, etc.).  
   • No extra spaces or characters—each line must contain ONLY the address.  
   • The file must end with exactly one trailing newline, and contain no blank lines.

That’s it—one concise command (a pipe is fine) should be enough. You do not need sudo or any new software. When the file is in place with the precise format above, the task is complete.
truth:
Pre-existing items  
• Directory: /home/user/network  (already present, writable by the current unprivileged user)  
• File: /home/user/network/ping_report.log  (size ~1.2 KB, Unix LF line endings)  
  Contents:  
  PING google.com (8.8.8.8): 56 data bytes  
  64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=0 ttl=117 time=11.813 ms  
  64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=1 ttl=117 time=10.978 ms  
  Request timeout for icmp_seq 2  
  
  ---  
  PING cloudflare.com (1.1.1.1): 56 data bytes  
  64 bytes from 1.1.1.1: icmp_seq=0 ttl=58 time=7.216 ms  
  Request timeout for icmp_seq 1  
  64 bytes from 1.1.1.1: icmp_seq=2 ttl=58 time=7.143 ms  
  
  ---  
  PING invalidhost (203.0.113.10): 56 data bytes  
  Request timeout for icmp_seq 0  
  Request timeout for icmp_seq 1  
  
  ---  
  From 192.168.0.1 icmp_seq=3 Destination Host Unreachable  
  (file terminates with a single LF)

Ground-truth expected after agent finishes  
• File: /home/user/network/successful_ips.txt  (permissions 0644, owner user:user)  
  Exact content (including final newline, shown here as ␤):  
  1.1.1.1␤  
  8.8.8.8␤
